genre.reasoning: Thesis-driven comment advocating for the FAIR principles, with substantial explanatory background.

thematic_thrust: Four foundational principles—FAIR—guide contemporary data publishing to enhance discovery and reuse by humans and machines.

characters.FAIR Guiding Principles: framework emphasizing Findability, Accessibility, Interoperability, Reusability
characters.Researchers: creators of scientific data seeking to share, cite, and reuse datasets
characters.Funding agencies: stakeholders requiring data stewardship and long-term management plans
characters.Publishers: organizations facilitating formal scholarly communication and data publication
characters.Data repositories: institutions storing and providing access to datasets globally
characters.Computational agents: automated systems tasked with autonomous data retrieval and integration
characters.FORCE11: Future of Research Communications and e-Scholarship community
characters.wwPDB: Worldwide Protein Data Bank for 3D protein structures
characters.UniProt: comprehensive protein sequence and annotation resource
characters.Dataverse: open-source data repository software deployed globally
characters.FAIRDOM: FAIR data and model management facility for systems biology
characters.ISA: community metadata tracking framework for life science datasets
characters.Open PHACTS: data integration platform for drug discovery information
characters.Machine-actionability: autonomous capacity to interpret and appropriately act on digital objects
characters.Metadata: structured information describing content, context, and quality of data
characters.Provenance: documented record of data origin, processing, and dependencies
characters.DOI: persistent globally unique identifier for digital objects

voice_anchors.snippet: The reason that we often need several weeks (or months) of specialist technical effort to gather the data necessary to answer such research questions is not the lack of appropriate technology; the reason is, that we do not pay our valuable digital objects the careful attention they deserve when we create and preserve them.
voice_anchors.note: Diagnostic rebuttal: corrects naive assumption, elevates care and stewardship as moral imperatives

voice_anchors.snippet: good data management and stewardship, therefore, are high quality digital publications that facilitate and simplify this ongoing process of discovery, evaluation, and reuse in downstream studies.
voice_anchors.note: Purpose-driven definition: frames stewardship through downstream outcomes and community benefit

voice_anchors.snippet: One of the grand challenges of data-intensive science, therefore, is to improve knowledge discovery through assisting both humans, and their computational agents, in the discovery of, access to, and integration and analysis of, task-appropriate scientific data
voice_anchors.note: Systemic register: invokes grand challenges, balances human and machine as equal stakeholders

concepts.label: Current data ecosystem prevents research reuse
concepts.snippet: existing digital ecosystem surrounding scholarly data publication prevents us from extracting maximum benefit from our research investments

concepts.label: FAIR principles guide data producers and publishers
concepts.snippet: four foundational principles—Findability, Accessibility, Interoperability, and Reusability—that serve to guide data producers and publishers

concepts.label: Principles apply beyond data to tools and algorithms
concepts.snippet: principles apply not only to ‘data’ in the conventional sense, but also

concepts.label: Good data management remains undefined and delegated
concepts.snippet: studies. What constitutes ‘good data management’ is, however, largely undefined, and is generally left

concepts.label: Multiple human stakeholders need data discovery and reuse
concepts.snippet: researchers wanting to share, get credit

concepts.label: Computational agents are critical overlooked stakeholders
concepts.snippet: Similar problems are encountered by the applications and computational agents that we task to undertake data retrieval and analysis on our behalf.

concepts.label: Specialized repositories serve only specific data types
concepts.snippet: there are well-curated, deeply-integrated, special-purpose repositories such as Genbank, Worldwide Protein Data Bank (wwPDB), and UniProt

concepts.label: General-purpose repositories fragment and decentralize data
concepts.snippet: The resulting data ecosystem, therefore, appears to be moving away from centralization, is becoming more diverse, and less integrated

concepts.label: Researcher cannot locate domain-specific datasets
concepts.snippet: The researcher is interested in comparing the alternatively-polyadenylated genes in this local dataset, to other examples of alternative-polyadenylation

concepts.label: Data integration requires months of specialist effort
concepts.snippet: The reason that we often need several weeks (or months) of specialist technical effort to gather the data necessary to answer such research questions

concepts.label: Root cause: insufficient care in data stewardship
concepts.snippet: the reason is, that we do not pay our valuable digital objects the careful attention they deserve when we create and preserve them.

concepts.label: Scholarly data must become first-class publication objects
concepts.snippet: goal is for scholarly digital objects of all kinds to become ‘first class

concepts.label: Leiden 2014 workshop convened diverse stakeholders
concepts.snippet: a workshop was held in Leiden, Netherlan

concepts.label: FAIR principles emerged from stakeholder deliberation
concepts.snippet: From the deliberations at the workshop the notion emerged that, through the definition of, and widespread support for, a minimal set of community-agreed guiding principles

concepts.label: Humans and machines face fundamentally distinct data barriers
concepts.snippet: Humans and machines often face distinct barriers when attempting to find and process data on the Web.

concepts.label: Humans possess semantic intuition; machines require explicit metadata
concepts.snippet: are capable of identifying and interpreting a wide variety of contextual cues

concepts.label: Machine-actionability exists on a spectrum of capability
concepts.snippet: a machine may be capable of determining the data-type of a discovered digital object, but not capable of parsing it due to it being in an unknown format

concepts.label: Bespoke parsers for each data type are unsustainable
concepts.snippet: Creating bespoke parsers, in all computer languages, for all data-types and all analytical tools that require those data-types, is not a sustainable activity.

concepts.label: FAIR specifies four detailed requirements with sub-principles
concepts.snippet:
Findable:
F1. (meta)data are assigned a globally

concepts.label: FAIR is domain-independent and modular by design
concepts.snippet: FAIR differs in that it describes concise, domain-independent, high-level principles that can be applied to a wide range of scholarly outputs.

concepts.label: Diverse repositories demonstrate FAIR in practice
concepts.snippet: Dataverse is an open-source data repository software installed in dozens of institutions globally to support public community repositories

concepts.label: Broader community converging on shared FAIR vision
concepts.snippet: Clearly, the broader community of stakeholders is coalescing around a set of common, dovetailed visions spanning all facets of the scholarly data publishing ecosystem.

concepts.label: Call for universal adoption and FAIR implementation
concepts.snippet: We call on all data producers and publishers to examine and implement these principles, and actively participate with the FAIR initiative
